title: Airtable API keys should not be disclosed
description: Airtable API keys should not be hardcoded in source code. These credentials include Airtable API keys, Personal Access Tokens (PATs), and Base IDs that provide access to Airtable databases and records. When exposed in code repositories, they can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data stored in Airtable bases and potential data manipulation or extraction.
recommendation: Store Airtable API credentials securely using environment variables, secure configuration management, or Airtable's recommended secure storage practices. Never commit API keys, Personal Access Tokens, or Base IDs directly to source code repositories.
impact: Critical security risk - unauthorized access to Airtable databases, sensitive records, and potential data manipulation or extraction

compliant.description: Using environment variable for Airtable API key

compliant.code:
# Use secure configuration management
api_key = config.get_secret('airtable_api_key')

checks.message: Airtable API key detected in source code
Airtable Personal Access Token detected in source code
Airtable Base ID detected in source code
